records.ety: [AS. und<?/n. See 1st Un-, and Do to perform.]

defs: To reverse, as what has been done; to annul; to bring to naught.
quotes: What's done can not be undone.
To-morrow, ere the setting sun, She 'd all undo that she had done.

defs: To loose; to open; to take to piece; to unfasten; to untie; hence, to unravel; to solve; as, to undo a knot; to undo a puzzling question; to undo a riddle.
quotes: She took the spindle, and undoing the thread gradually, measured it.

defs: To bring to poverty; to impoverish; to ruin, as in reputation, morals, hopes, or the like; as, many are undone by unavoidable losses, but more undo themselves by vices and dissipation, or by indolence.
quotes: That quaffing and drinking will undo you,
